title: didimpute — project instructions
text: didimpute R package: faithful port Python did_imputation package (Georgii Marinichev’s implementation, https://github.com/Gmarinichev/did_imputation) Borusyak, Jaravel & Spiess (2024) difference--differences imputation estimator, plus event_plot. Faithfulness Python implementation’s numerical output — original Stata package, independent Kyle Butts’s separate CRAN didimputation.

title: didimpute — project instructions
text: Package (shippable): ~/projects/software/didimpute (repo). Port workspace (part package): ~/projects/claude/didimpute_port — Python .venv, golden-grid generator (code/gen_golden.py, code/make_panels.py), saved golden CSVs, design spec, implementation plan, SDD progress ledger. Mirrors drlate_port precedent.

title: didimpute — project instructions
text: fixest::demean() analog Python’s pyhdfe.residualize() (HDFE absorption untreated subsample). stats::lm.wfit recovers control coefficients. BJS-specific pieces (FE-level recovery, imputation-weight iteration, smartweight/influence-function SEs) hand-translated — R library implements . data.table throughout. NOTE recurring traps already handled: get(col) mis-resolves inside dt[...] column shares name (capture column-name strings / use dt[[col]]); never rely merge row order (re-extract vectors merge); never-treated row’s Rel_time NA R (Python NaN) — every Rel_time-derived indicator MUST guard NA -> 0.

title: didimpute — project instructions
text: did_impute.R — orchestrator: prep, delta detection, untreated mask, imputation, aggregation, df_a, SE wiring, combined $V assembly. (Largest file.) recover_fe.R — iterative FE-level recovery (alternating weighted means). imputation_weights.R — update_weights, imputation_weights (BJS weight iteration). standard_errors.R — compute_effect_se (smartweight), compute_controls_se, compute_pretrends, .compute_df_a. output.R — S3 print/summary. event_plot.R — ggplot2 event-study plot.

title: didimpute — project instructions
text: Correctness enforced Python golden grid: 14 configurations matched Python package — estimates standard errors — ~1e-6 (1e-8). Tests live tests/testthat/; golden CSVs tests/testthat/fixtures/. regenerate golden grid: ~/projects/claude/didimpute_port, source .venv/bin/activate python code/gen_golden.py, copy output/golden/*_result.csv (+ *_data.csv) tests/testthat/fixtures/. did_imputation(...) call MUST immediately preceded np.random.seed(1) (matches R seed=1 one randomized rank-check path agrees). shared panel 150 units minn=30 suppress horizon estimands. shrink . Run tests: Rscript -e 'devtools::load_all(); testthat::test_local()'. R CMD check clean (warning vignette build needing Pandoc PATH, environmental).

title: didimpute — project instructions
text: $V full cluster-robust covariance matrix reported estimands (effects, pretrends, controls), sqrt(diag(V)) reproduces SEs. deliberate improvement upstream Python package, whose V scalar (sum squared SEs). one deliberate deviation Python: randomized rank/collinearity check seeded via seed argument (default 1L) reproducibility. Absorbed degrees freedom (df_a) cluster-robust SEs exact default two-way FE / cluster--unit case ≤2 fixed effects; >2 non-nested fixed effects value approximate warning emitted. Unimplemented options (faithful Python — error): timecontrols, unitcontrols, leaveoneout, hetby, project. fe=NULL maps two-way FE c(,t); Python intercept-(_const_) path implemented (golden case needs ).

title: didimpute — project instructions
text: package built via subagent-driven TDD golden grid. change touches estimator MUST keep golden grid matching 1e-6 — diverges, debug Python (temporary dumps gen_golden.py), never loosen tolerance pass. Docs deploy via pkgdown; README links pkgdown site. Re-run devtools::document() roxygen edits.

title: Getting started with didimpute
text: didimpute implements imputation estimator Borusyak, Jaravel Spiess (2024, Review Economic Studies) staggered difference--differences () designs. estimator proceeds two steps: Fit two-way fixed effects (TWFE) model untreated observations obtain counterfactual prediction ŷ(0)\hat{y}_{}(0) treated observation. Aggregate unit-level treatment effects τ̂=yit−ŷ(0)\hat{\tau}_{} = y_{} - \hat{y}_{}(0) researcher-specified estimands (average treatment effect treated, event-study coefficients, etc.). Standard errors cluster-robust use smartweights influence-function approach original Python package.

title: Getting started with didimpute
code: remotes::install_github("xiangao/didimpute")

title: Getting started with didimpute
text: estimator requires long-format panel : : unit identifier t: time variable (integer-valued) Ei: first treated period (NA never-treated units) y: outcome variable
code: library(didimpute)  # Synthetic staggered panel: 30 units, 10 periods set.seed(42) n_units <- 30; n_t <- 10  panel <- expand.grid(i = seq_len(n_units), t = seq_len(n_t))  # Three treatment cohorts: early (t=5), late (t=7), never-treated panel$Ei <- ifelse(panel$i <= 10, 5L,               ifelse(panel$i <= 20, 7L, NA_integer_))  # DGP: true effect = 1.5, with cohort heterogeneity panel$y <- 1.5 * (!is.na(panel$Ei) & panel$t >= panel$Ei) +              0.5 * (panel$i <= 10) * (!is.na(panel$Ei) & panel$t >= panel$Ei) +              rnorm(nrow(panel), sd = 0.5)

title: Getting started with didimpute
text: simplest call returns average treatment effect treated (ATT) pooled across treated observations.
code: res_ate <- did_impute(panel, y = "y", i = "i", t = "t", Ei = "Ei") #> The number of treated entities is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong, consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. print(res_ate) #> <didimpute did_impute result> #>   Effects: tau_ate=1.741  #>   SE     : tau_ate=0.08701  #>   N obs: 300

title: Getting started with didimpute
text: Pass horizons estimate separate effects relative-time horizon. Use pretrends also estimate placebo pre-trend coefficients. summary returns tidy data.frame one row per estimand: post-treatment effects (tau0, tau1, tau2) followed pre-trend placebo coefficients (pre1, pre2).
code: res <- did_impute(   panel,   y         = "y",   i         = "i",   t         = "t",   Ei        = "Ei",   horizons  = 0:2,   pretrends = 2 ) #> WARNING: suppressing wtr0, consider lower minn or minn=0. #> WARNING: suppressing wtr1, consider lower minn or minn=0. #> WARNING: suppressing wtr2, consider lower minn or minn=0.  summary(res) #>   term    estimate std.error #> 1 tau0  0.00000000 0.0000000 #> 2 tau1  0.00000000 0.0000000 #> 3 tau2  0.00000000 0.0000000 #> 4 pre1 -0.06783874 0.1673345 #> 5 pre2  0.19735697 0.1585843

title: Getting started with didimpute
text: event_plot() produces ggplot2 figure pre-trends left post-treatment effects right.  rarea variant draws shaded confidence band instead error bars:
code: if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   event_plot(res) } if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   event_plot(res, plot_type = "rarea") }

title: Getting started with didimpute
text: hbalance = TRUE, estimator restricts cohorts observed every requested horizon, ensuring balanced event-study sample.
code: res_bal <- did_impute(   panel,   y        = "y",   i        = "i",   t        = "t",   Ei       = "Ei",   horizons = 0:2,   hbalance = TRUE ) #> WARNING: suppressing wtr0, consider lower minn or minn=0. #> WARNING: suppressing wtr1, consider lower minn or minn=0. #> WARNING: suppressing wtr2, consider lower minn or minn=0. summary(res_bal) #>   term estimate std.error #> 1 tau0        0         0 #> 2 tau1        0         0 #> 3 tau2        0         0

title: Getting started with didimpute
code: res_all <- did_impute(   panel,   y           = "y",   i           = "i",   t           = "t",   Ei          = "Ei",   allhorizons = TRUE ) #> WARNING: suppressing wtr0, consider lower minn or minn=0. #> WARNING: suppressing wtr1, consider lower minn or minn=0. #> WARNING: suppressing wtr2, consider lower minn or minn=0. #> WARNING: suppressing wtr3, consider lower minn or minn=0. #> WARNING: suppressing wtr4, consider lower minn or minn=0. #> WARNING: suppressing wtr5, consider lower minn or minn=0. summary(res_all) #>   term estimate std.error #> 1 tau0        0         0 #> 2 tau1        0         0 #> 3 tau2        0         0 #> 4 tau3        0         0 #> 5 tau4        0         0 #> 6 tau5        0         0

title: Getting started with didimpute
text: Pass name weight column aw:
code: panel$pop <- runif(nrow(panel), 0.5, 1.5)  # synthetic population weight res_w <- did_impute(   panel,   y  = "y",   i  = "i",   t  = "t",   Ei = "Ei",   aw = "pop" ) #> The number of treated entities is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong, consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. print(res_w) #> <didimpute did_impute result> #>   Effects: tau_ate=1.751  #>   SE     : tau_ate=0.09118  #>   N obs: 300

title: Getting started with didimpute
text: large panels, passing nose = TRUE skips (expensive) influence-function SE computation.
code: res_nose <- did_impute(   panel,   y    = "y",   i    = "i",   t    = "t",   Ei   = "Ei",   nose = TRUE ) #> The number of treated entities is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong, consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. print(res_nose) #> <didimpute did_impute result> #>   Effects: tau_ate=1.741  #>   N obs: 300

title: Getting started with didimpute
text: returned object includes square covariance matrix V dimension K×KK \times K, KK total number reported estimands (effects, pre-trends, controls, order). Row column names match estimand names. sqrt(diag(res$V)) reproduces reported per-estimand standard errors numerical precision. deliberate improvement upstream Python package, whose V scalar (sum squared standard errors). V NULL nose = TRUE standard-error components present.

title: Getting started with didimpute
text: following arguments match Python package API yet implemented raise error supplied: timecontrols, unitcontrols, leaveoneout, hetby, project.

title: Difference-in-Differences Imputation Estimator (Borusyak, Jaravel & Spiess)
text: R port Georgii Marinichev’s Python did_imputation package, implementing imputation estimator Borusyak, Jaravel Spiess (2024, Review Economic Studies) staggered difference--differences designs. (separate, independent R implementation also available didimputation Kyle Butts.) Full documentation: https://xiangao.github.io/didimpute

title: Difference-in-Differences Imputation Estimator (Borusyak, Jaravel & Spiess)
code: # Install from GitHub remotes::install_github("xiangao/didimpute")

title: Difference-in-Differences Imputation Estimator (Borusyak, Jaravel & Spiess)
text: example constructs minimal synthetic staggered panel estimates event-study effects horizons 0, 1, 2, two pre-trend placebo coefficients.
code: library(didimpute)  # Synthetic staggered panel: 20 units, 10 periods set.seed(42) n_units <- 20; n_t <- 10 panel <- expand.grid(i = seq_len(n_units), t = seq_len(n_t))  # Units 1–10 treated at t = 6; units 11–20 are never treated panel$Ei <- ifelse(panel$i <= 10, 6L, NA_integer_)  # DGP: treatment effect of 1.0 in post periods panel$y <- 1.0 * (!is.na(panel$Ei) & panel$t >= panel$Ei) +              rnorm(nrow(panel), sd = 0.5)  # Estimate event study: horizons 0–2, two pre-trend placebo tests res <- did_impute(   panel,   y        = "y",   i        = "i",   t        = "t",   Ei       = "Ei",   horizons = 0:2,   pretrends = 2 )  # Tidy summary table summary(res)  # Event-study plot (requires ggplot2) if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   event_plot(res) }

title: Difference-in-Differences Imputation Estimator (Borusyak, Jaravel & Spiess)
text: Borusyak, K., Jaravel, X., Spiess, J. (2024). Revisiting Event-Study Designs: Robust Efficient Estimation. Review Economic Studies, 91(6), 3253–3285.

what: Compute cluster-robust SEs for control-variable coefficients (influence function) — compute_controls_se
title: Compute cluster-robust SEs for control-variable coefficients (influence function) — compute_controls_se
text: Faithful port Python compute_controls_se (did_imputation.py lines 351-404, 691-695).  control c: demean c controls FE (untreated subsample, dropping singleton FE levels); regress demeaned c demeaned others; form per-observation ctrlweight residuals; cluster- sum ctrlweight * imput_resid; sandwich V = M'M.

title: Compute cluster-robust SEs for control-variable coefficients (influence function) — compute_controls_se
code: compute_controls_se(dt, controls, fe, cluster, aw, df_a, beta)

title: Compute cluster-robust SEs for control-variable coefficients (influence function) — compute_controls_se
text: dt data.table containing untreated treated rows (needs imput_resid untreated rows) controls character vector control column names fe character vector FE column names cluster name cluster column aw name analytic weight column, NULL df_a absorbed degrees freedom (.compute_df_a) beta named numeric vector WLS coefficients control

title: Compute cluster-robust SEs for control-variable coefficients (influence function) — compute_controls_se
text: named numeric vector cluster-robust SEs, one per control

what: Compute cluster-robust standard errors via BJS smartweights — compute_effect_se
title: Compute cluster-robust standard errors via BJS smartweights — compute_effect_se
text: Faithful port Python compute_effect_se (did_imputation.py lines 615-711, -controls path). Operates COPY dt (caller must pass one).

title: Compute cluster-robust standard errors via BJS smartweights — compute_effect_se
code: compute_effect_se(   dt,   wtr,   y,   cluster,   avgeffectsby,   gr_var,   fe,   controls = character() )

title: Compute cluster-robust standard errors via BJS smartweights — compute_effect_se
text: dt data.table (modified; pass copy) wtr character vector original treatment-weight column names y name outcome column cluster name cluster column avgeffectsby character vector columns defining estimand group gr_var character vector = union cluster + avgeffectsby fe character vector fixed-effect columns (passed imputation_weights) controls character vector control variable names (default empty)

title: Compute cluster-robust standard errors via BJS smartweights — compute_effect_se
text: list se (named numeric), group_sums matrix, V matrix

title: Compute cluster-robust standard errors via BJS smartweights — compute_effect_se
text: weight column wtr: Fill NaN copy original w (non-iterated weights) Compute smartweights treated rows Residual: y - y_hat (rows), overridden tau - avg_taus (treated rows) product = resid * copy * wei group_sums = sum(product) per cluster V = G'G G = cbind(group_sums); ses = sqrt(diag(V)).

what: Compute cluster-robust standard errors for pretrend coefficients — compute_pretrends
title: Compute cluster-robust standard errors for pretrend coefficients — compute_pretrends
text: Faithful port Python pretrends block (did_imputation.py lines 477-566, 700-704).  Operates untreated subsample dropping singleton FE levels.  h=1..pretrends: residualise pretrendvar_h FE (pretrend/control vars); WLS get preresid; form per-observation preweight; normalise; cluster-sum preweight * preresid * sqrt(dof_adj).  Sandwich V = M'M.

title: Compute cluster-robust standard errors for pretrend coefficients — compute_pretrends
code: compute_pretrends(dt, y, fe, cluster, controls, pretrends, aw)

title: Compute cluster-robust standard errors for pretrend coefficients — compute_pretrends
text: dt data.table (rows, including treated — untreated filter applied inside) y name outcome column fe character vector FE column names cluster name cluster column controls character vector control column names pretrends integer number pretrend horizons (h = 1..pretrends) aw name analytic weight column, NULL

title: Compute cluster-robust standard errors for pretrend coefficients — compute_pretrends
text: list coefs (named numeric) ses (named numeric), plus list_pre_weps (matrix per-cluster influence vectors)

title: Difference-in-differences imputation estimator (BJS 2024) — did_impute
text: Implements imputation estimator Borusyak, Jaravel Spiess (2024, AER) staggered difference--differences designs.  estimator imputes treated unit's counterfactual outcome two-way fixed effects model fit untreated observations, aggregates resulting unit-level treatment effects user-specified estimands.  Standard errors cluster-robust (clustered unit default) using smartweights influence-function approach original Python package.

title: Difference-in-differences imputation estimator (BJS 2024) — did_impute
code: did_impute(   df,   y,   i,   t,   Ei,   controls = character(),   fe = NULL,   timecontrols = character(),   aw = NULL,   unitcontrols = character(),   wtr = character(),   sum = FALSE,   horizons = NULL,   allhorizons = FALSE,   hbalance = FALSE,   hetby = character(),   project = character(),   minn = 30,   saveweights = FALSE,   shift = 0,   pretrends = 0,   cluster = "",   avgeffectsby = character(),   leaveoneout = FALSE,   nose = FALSE,   delta = NULL,   seed = 1L )

title: Difference-in-differences imputation estimator (BJS 2024) — did_impute
text: df data frame (data.table) long format. y Character. Name outcome column. Character. Name unit identifier column. t Character. Name time column (must integer-valued coercible integer). Ei Character. Name treatment-timing column.  NA indicates never-treated unit; finite integer first treated period. controls Character vector time-varying control variable names (default: none). fe Character vector fixed-effect column names.  Defaults c(, t) (standard two-way FE). timecontrols yet implemented; passing non-empty vector raises error. aw Character. Name analytic-weight column, NULL (default). unitcontrols yet implemented; passing non-empty vector raises error. wtr Character vector user-supplied treatment-weight column names. combined horizons, allhorizons, hbalance. sum Logical. TRUE, sum effects rather averaging (weights normalised sum 1 treated rows).  Default FALSE. horizons Numeric vector relative-time horizons estimate event-study effects (e.g. 0:3). allhorizons Logical.  TRUE, estimate effects observed relative-time horizons (inferred data).  combined horizons. hbalance Logical.  TRUE horizons specified, restrict cohorts observed every horizon horizons (horizon-balanced sample). hetby yet implemented; passing non-empty vector raises error. project yet implemented; passing non-empty vector raises error. minn Minimum effective sample size (default 30).  estimand suppressed (weight set zero) effective N falls threshold.  Set 0 disable. saveweights Logical.  TRUE, include per-observation treatment weights returned object (yet used downstream methods; included API parity Python package). shift Integer.  Time shift applied constructing relative time untreated indicator (default 0). pretrends Non-negative integer.  Number pre-treatment horizons estimate placebo ("pre-trend") coefficients.  Default 0 (none). cluster Character. Name cluster column cluster-robust standard errors.  Defaults (cluster unit). avgeffectsby Character vector.  Columns define estimand grouping used smartweights influence function.  Defaults c(Ei, t). leaveoneout yet implemented; setting TRUE raises error. nose Logical.  TRUE, skip standard error computation. Estimates still returned.  Default FALSE. delta Integer NULL.  Time-step size.  NULL (default) step detected automatically modal first difference observed time values. seed Integer seed passed set.seed randomised rank / collinearity check control variables.  Setting fixed seed makes check reproducible across calls.  one deliberate deviation upstream Python package, uses non-seeded random draw.  Default 1L.

title: Difference-in-differences imputation estimator (BJS 2024) — did_impute
text: S3 object class "did_impute" following components: estimates Named list point estimates.  baseline case (horizons, user wtr) produces single element tau_ate.  Horizon estimates named tau0, tau1, ...; user-weight estimates use weight column names. std_errors Named list cluster-robust standard errors, matching estimates.  NULL nose = TRUE. pretrends_estimates Named list pre-trend (placebo) coefficients (pre1, pre2, ...).  NULL pretrends = 0. pretrends_std_errors Named list cluster-robust SEs pre-trend coefficients.  NULL pretrends = 0 nose = TRUE. controls_estimates Named list WLS coefficients controls.  NULL controls empty. controls_std_errors Named list cluster-robust SEs control coefficients.  NULL controls empty nose = TRUE. n_obs Total observation count entering estimand computation (untreated rows plus treated rows non-zero weight). V square covariance matrix dimension \(K \times K\), \(K\) total number reported estimands (effects, pre-trends, controls, order).  Row column names match estimand names.  sqrt(diag(V)) reproduces reported per-estimand standard errors numerical precision.  deliberate improvement upstream Python package, whose V scalar (sum squared SEs).  NULL nose = TRUE standard-error components present. weights Reserved future use (NULL unless saveweights = TRUE requested; field included API parity Python package).

title: Difference-in-differences imputation estimator (BJS 2024) — did_impute
text: Absorbed degrees freedom.  cluster-robust SE scaling factor uses absorbed degrees freedom (\(df_a\)) computed port pyhdfe pairwise method: FE nested within cluster variable dropped; remaining FEs contribute unique-level counts minus number bipartite connected components shared across pairs. exact default two-way FE / cluster--unit case configuration two non-nested fixed effects.  two non-nested fixed effects value approximation warning emitted. Unimplemented options.  arguments timecontrols, unitcontrols, leaveoneout, hetby, project match Python package API yet implemented; raise error supplied.

title: Difference-in-differences imputation estimator (BJS 2024) — did_impute
text: Borusyak, K., Jaravel, X., Spiess, J. (2024). Revisiting Event-Study Designs: Robust Efficient Estimation. Review Economic Studies, 91(6), 3253–3285.

title: Difference-in-differences imputation estimator (BJS 2024) — did_impute
code: # Minimal synthetic staggered panel (4 units, 6 periods) set.seed(42) n_units <- 4; n_t <- 6 panel <- expand.grid(i = seq_len(n_units), t = seq_len(n_t)) panel$Ei <- ifelse(panel$i <= 2, 4L, NA_integer_)  # units 1-2 treated at t=4 panel$y  <- 0.5 * (!is.na(panel$Ei) & panel$t >= panel$Ei) +               rnorm(nrow(panel), sd = 0.2)  # Baseline ATT estimate res <- did_impute(panel, y = "y", i = "i", t = "t", Ei = "Ei") #> WARNING: suppressing wtr, consider lower minn or minn=0. #> The number of treated entities is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong, consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. print(res) #> <didimpute did_impute result> #>   Effects: tau_ate=0  #>   SE     : tau_ate=0  #>   N obs: 18   # Event-study with horizons 0 and 1 and one pre-period res2 <- did_impute(panel, y = "y", i = "i", t = "t", Ei = "Ei",                    horizons = 0:1, pretrends = 1, minn = 0) #> The number of treated entities for 'wtr0' is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong; consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. #> The number of treated entities for 'wtr1' is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong; consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. summary(res2) #>   term    estimate std.error #> 1 tau0  0.38111364 0.1955443 #> 2 tau1  0.41610601 0.2809816 #> 3 pre1 -0.09815805 0.1807401

what: Compute absorbed degrees of freedom matching pyhdfe pairwise method — .compute_df_a
title: Compute absorbed degrees of freedom matching pyhdfe pairwise method — .compute_df_a
text: Mirrors pyhdfe algorithm.degrees logic (used cluster_ids): FE dropped nested within (.e., levels refinement ) cluster group.  remaining FEs contribute unique level counts, minus number connected components across pairs (pairwise method).

title: Compute absorbed degrees of freedom matching pyhdfe pairwise method — .compute_df_a
code: .compute_df_a(dt, fe, cluster)

title: Compute absorbed degrees of freedom matching pyhdfe pairwise method — .compute_df_a
text: dt data.table restricted untreated subsample fe character vector FE column names cluster name cluster column

title: Compute absorbed degrees of freedom matching pyhdfe pairwise method — .compute_df_a
text: integer absorbed degrees freedom

title: Compute absorbed degrees of freedom matching pyhdfe pairwise method — .compute_df_a
text: standard case (fe = c(, t), cluster = ): -FE nested within -cluster, t-FE contributes => df_a = uniqueN(t).

what: Event-study plot for a did_impute result — event_plot
title: Event-study plot for a did_impute result — event_plot
text: Produces ggplot2 event-study figure did_impute result object explicitly supplied coefficient standard-error lists. Pre-trend estimates keyed pre<k> placed \(x = -k\); horizon estimates keyed tau<k> \(x = +k\).  Confidence intervals use critical value \(z_{1-\alpha/2}\).

title: Event-study plot for a did_impute result — event_plot
code: event_plot(   results_obj = NULL,   pretrends = NULL,   pretrends_std = NULL,   effects = NULL,   effects_std = NULL,   plot_type = c("rcap", "rarea"),   significance_level = 0.05,   together = FALSE,   xlab = "Time Relative to Treatment",   ylab = "Coefficient",   title = NULL,   pretrends_color = "blue",   effects_color = "red",   ... )

title: Event-study plot for a did_impute result — event_plot
text: results_obj did_impute result object, NULL. pretrends Named list pre-trend estimates (keys pre<k>). Used results_obj NULL. pretrends_std Named list pre-trend standard errors. effects Named list effect estimates (keys tau<k>). effects_std Named list effect standard errors. plot_type "rcap" (error bars, default) "rarea" (shaded ribbon). significance_level Significance level CIs (default 0.05, giving 95 percent CIs). together Logical; TRUE pre-trends effects combined single series (default FALSE). xlab X-axis label. ylab Y-axis label. title Optional plot title. pretrends_color Colour pre-trend series (default "blue"). effects_color Colour effects series (default "red"). ... Currently unused.

title: Event-study plot for a did_impute result — event_plot
text: ggplot object.  underlying data frame (accessible p$data) contains columns x, coef, err, group.

title: Event-study plot for a did_impute result — event_plot
code: # Minimal synthetic panel for event-study set.seed(42) n_units <- 4; n_t <- 6 df <- expand.grid(i = seq_len(n_units), t = seq_len(n_t)) df$Ei <- ifelse(df$i <= 2, 4L, NA_integer_) df$y <- 0.5 * (!is.na(df$Ei) & df$t >= df$Ei) + rnorm(nrow(df), sd = 0.2)  res <- did_impute(df, y = "y", i = "i", t = "t", Ei = "Ei",                   horizons = 0:2, pretrends = 2, minn = 0) #> The number of treated entities for 'wtr0' is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong; consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. #> The number of treated entities for 'wtr1' is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong; consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. #> The number of treated entities for 'wtr2' is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong; consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. event_plot(res)  event_plot(res, plot_type = "rarea", together = TRUE)

title: Print a did_impute result — print.did_impute
code: set.seed(1) panel <- expand.grid(i = 1:4, t = 1:6) panel$Ei <- ifelse(panel$i <= 2, 4L, NA_integer_) panel$y  <- 0.3 * (!is.na(panel$Ei) & panel$t >= panel$Ei) + rnorm(nrow(panel), sd = 0.1) res <- did_impute(panel, y = "y", i = "i", t = "t", Ei = "Ei") #> WARNING: suppressing wtr, consider lower minn or minn=0. #> The number of treated entities is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong, consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. print(res) #> <didimpute did_impute result> #>   Effects: tau_ate=0  #>   SE     : tau_ate=0  #>   N obs: 18

title: Summarise a did_impute result — summary.did_impute
code: set.seed(1) panel <- expand.grid(i = 1:4, t = 1:6) panel$Ei <- ifelse(panel$i <= 2, 4L, NA_integer_) panel$y  <- 0.3 * (!is.na(panel$Ei) & panel$t >= panel$Ei) + rnorm(nrow(panel), sd = 0.1) res <- did_impute(panel, y = "y", i = "i", t = "t", Ei = "Ei",                   horizons = 0:1, pretrends = 2, minn = 0) #> The number of treated entities for 'wtr0' is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong; consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. #> The number of treated entities for 'wtr1' is too small for some cohorts. Standard Errors may be wrong; consider using avgeffectsby option, averaging the the effect by treated X post variable. summary(res) #>   term    estimate  std.error #> 1 tau0  0.18004283 0.07299948 #> 2 tau1  0.35146589 0.05508206 #> 3 pre1 -0.02143844 0.19970161 #> 4 pre2 -0.02571258 0.14878493
